content.problemStatement:
You are given three positive integers N,L,N,L,N,L, and RRR.

Find a permutation‚ÄÝ^\dag‚ÄÝ PPP of length NNN with **minimum** number of indices iii satisfying L‚â§‚àëj=1iPj‚â§R\displaystyle{L \le \sum_{j=1}^{i} P_j \le R}L‚â§j=1‚àëi‚ÄãPj‚Äã‚â§R.

In case multiple such permutations exist, you may print any.

‚ÄÝ^\dag‚ÄÝA permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains three positive integers N,L,N,L,N,L, and RRR ‚àí-‚àí the length of PPP and the upper and lower bounds.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, print NNN distinct positive integers, representing permutation PPP.


If there exists multiple PPP, you can print any one.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

It can be shown that for no permutation of length 333, ‚àëj=1iPj\sum_{j=1}^{i} P_j‚àëj=1i‚ÄãPj‚Äã lies in range [3,6][3, 6][3,6] for less than 222 indices.
Test case 333: Consider the permutation P=[2,3,1]P = [2, 3, 1]P=[2,3,1]. For i=1,i=2,i=1, i=2,i=1,i=2, and i=3i=3i=3, the value of ‚àëj=1iPj\sum_{j=1}^{i} P_j‚àëj=1i‚ÄãPj‚Äã is 2,5,2, 5,2,5, and 666 respectively, none of which lie in range [3,4][3, 4][3,4].
